reasoning: The question requires specifically "Explosions/Remote violence" events to exceed 10 times the baseline value. The 360-day baseline preceding May 25, 2025 is given as 1.000, meaning there was an average of 0.000 events per 30 days. To trigger a YES resolution, eSwatini would therefore need at least 11 Explosions/Remote violence events in the very specific 30-day period leading up to each resolution date. eSwatini historically has had negligible incidents of Explosions/Remote violence in the ACLED dataset (which primarily captures warfare-style incidents like IEDs, airstrikes, artillery, etc. for this category, distinct from riots where petrol bombs may be present). Generating 11+ remote explosive events in 30 days implies an unprecedented terrorist campaign or outbreak of war, none of which is indicated by current global conflict trackers or 2024/2025 news reports. Probabilities are set to the minimum allowed bound for near-term dates (0.05) and scale slightly upwards over the longer horizon to reflect growing uncertainty.
